skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Generate text-to-video clips with Wan 2.7 through the RunComfy CLI. The skill documents supported inputs, prompt guidance, audio-driven lip-sync, output handling, and model limitations.
skill.summary: Generate Wan 2.7 text-to-video jobs through RunComfy with prompt, audio, duration, resolution, and output guidance.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but most high-severity matches are Markdown or placeholder false positives. The real risks are intended behavior: invoking the local RunComfy CLI, sending prompts and media URLs to RunComfy over HTTPS, downloading generated outputs, and storing or reading an API token. No prompt injection attempt, hidden exfiltration flow, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Local CLI Invocation With Remote Job Submission
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run the local RunComfy CLI for Wan 2.7 video generation. This is the intended function, but it executes an external command and submits user prompts to a remote model service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and remote POST behavior are explicitly documented. The risk is real but appears purposeful for a RunComfy video-generation skill, not hidden or malicious.

medium_findings.title: API Token Storage and Environment Token Use
medium_findings.description: The skill documents login and CI token handling, including token storage under the user config directory. Token handling is expected for authenticated API access, but mishandling could expose credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly documents token storage and RUNCOMFY_TOKEN usage. It also states owner-only permissions, which lowers severity but does not remove credential-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Media URLs and Generated Output Downloads
medium_findings.description: The skill supports audio URLs and describes downloading generated output from RunComfy domains. This can expose supplied media URLs and creates filesystem writes in the selected output directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and download behavior is documented in the security section. The stated endpoint whitelist and file size cap reduce but do not eliminate data exposure and filesystem-write concerns.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static external-command detections are Markdown inline code or fenced examples for documented CLI usage and input fields. They do not show hidden dynamic shell construction or user-controlled shell expansion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged ranges are documentation tables and prose, not executable source code. The security section states the prompt is passed as JSON and is not shell-expanded.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Path Traversal Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in ordinary Markdown text such as frontmatter, anti-pattern wording, and an exit-code heading. The path traversal match is an ellipsis placeholder in an example URL, not filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic API or path manipulation code is present in the reviewed file. These findings are caused by broad string matching against Markdown content.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on `runcomfy run` commands for its core behavior. Users should understand that the agent may invoke a local CLI and create output files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command examples are explicit and central to the skill. They are not concealed, but they are security-relevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Network Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents outbound HTTPS requests to RunComfy model endpoints and generated-output download domains.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact outbound endpoint behavior is documented. It is expected for a hosted model API skill, but it should remain visible to users.

content.value_statement: Creating polished short videos requires precise model inputs and reliable job handling. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Wan 2.7 prompts, RunComfy CLI calls, audio inputs, and output settings.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose Wan 2.7 instead of related video models.
Documents the RunComfy text-to-video endpoint and supported input fields.
Shows CLI invocation patterns for standard, audio-driven, and literal-control jobs.
Provides prompt guidance for camera motion, negative prompts, seeds, and prompt expansion.
Lists practical limits for duration, resolution, aspect ratio, audio files, and reference media.
Describes RunComfy token handling, outbound endpoints, output downloads, and common exit codes.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and an authenticated RunComfy account or token.
Generates clips up to 15 seconds with a maximum documented resolution of 1080p.
Sends prompts, audio URLs, and job data to RunComfy model infrastructure.
Does not provide in-pass voice generation; it uses a supplied audio URL for lip-sync.

use_cases.title: Create short product videos
use_cases.description: Generate concise product showcase clips with controlled camera movement, lighting, aspect ratio, and output duration.

use_cases.title: Produce lip-synced ad variants
use_cases.description: Use a supplied audio URL to create spokesperson clips and localized voiceover variants.

use_cases.title: Prototype cinematic motion prompts
use_cases.description: Test camera directions, seeds, negative prompts, and prompt expansion settings before scaling production work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard short clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5 second Wan 2.7 video of a product on a clean studio surface. Use 1080p, 16:9, and smooth camera motion.

prompt_templates.title: Vertical social video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 9:16 Wan 2.7 clip for social media. Show a barista making espresso with close-up handheld motion and warm morning light.

prompt_templates.title: Audio-driven spokesperson
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wan 2.7 with my audio URL to create a 12 second lip-synced spokesperson clip. Keep the camera locked and the lighting warm.

prompt_templates.title: Literal controlled variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that limits automatic prompt rewriting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Wan 2.7 with prompt expansion disabled. Preserve my exact shot description and use a negative prompt for subtitles, flicker, watermarks, and distorted hands.

output_examples.input: A request for a 5 second 1080p product showcase video.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a RunComfy Wan 2.7 job with the product prompt, default landscape aspect ratio, prompt expansion enabled, and a selected output directory.

output_examples.input: A request for a vertical lip-synced spokesperson clip with a voiceover file.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks audio duration and format, sets vertical aspect ratio, matches duration to the voiceover, and routes the job to the Wan 2.7 text-to-video endpoint.

output_examples.input: A request for strict brand wording and no automatic rewriting.
output_examples.output: The assistant disables prompt expansion, keeps the prompt literal, adds concrete negative prompts, and recommends reusing the seed for controlled variants.

best_practices: Start prompts with camera angle, motion, subject, and one primary action.
Use concrete negative prompts for visible defects such as subtitles, flicker, and watermarks.
Keep audio files within the documented format, size, and duration limits before running lip-sync jobs.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many unrelated actions in a single short clip prompt.
Do not use vague negative prompts such as broad quality complaints.
Do not send sensitive prompts or private media URLs unless RunComfy processing is acceptable.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate short Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips through the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects the RunComfy CLI to be installed and authenticated.

faq.question: Can it create lip-synced videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports an audio URL for lip-sync when the audio meets the documented requirements.

faq.question: What is the maximum documented clip length?
faq.answer: The skill documents a 15 second duration cap for Wan 2.7 jobs.

faq.question: Does the skill send data to an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends job inputs to RunComfy model endpoints and downloads generated outputs.

faq.question: When should prompt expansion be disabled?
faq.answer: Disable it when exact wording, brand constraints, or literal prompt control are more important than automatic enhancement.
